articles.title: Quantum ‘Time Trick’ Could Let Tiny Devices Sip Energy From Their Surroundings
articles.summary: Physicists have harnessed a quantum phenomenon called the non-linear Hall effect (NLHE) to convert electrical signals directly into current—no bulky components needed. It works at room temperature, but for now, it’s only suited for atom-thin, self-powered microchips.

articles.title: From Qualifying Loss to History: Potapova’s ‘Lucky Loser’ Run Stuns Madrid
articles.summary: Anastasia Potapova lost in qualifying but entered the Madrid Open as a lucky loser. She then beat former world No. 1 Karolina Pliskova 6-1, 6-7(4), 6-3 to become the first lucky loser in a WTA 1000 semifinal.

articles.title: Light Phone Opens Up: Now Anyone Can Build ‘Tools’ for Its Minimalist Handset
articles.summary: Light Phone is launching a developer program for its Light Phone III, letting users create simple apps called Tools. From guitar tuners to transit schedules, these add-ons aim to make the ‘dumb phone’ more useful without turning it into a smartphone.
